food_name: Big Drop Brewing Co. IPA Non-Alcoholic Beer (Can)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general beer class (FOODON_03301103); no specific non-alcoholic beer subclass exists. Closest parent match used; non-alcoholic variant noted in classification.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers beer and beer-based drinks broadly. No dedicated non-alcoholic beer code exists in FoodEx2 at this level; closest parent match used with non-alcoholic qualifier noted.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for non-alcoholic IPA beer as a food product. SNOMED CT coverage for packaged non-alcoholic beverages is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml can of Big Drop IPA non-alcoholic beer, equivalent to approximately 330 g, providing roughly 60 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Beverages, beer, regular, all (FDC ID 173190) and non-alcoholic beer reference entries
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Big Drop IPA is a branded non-alcoholic beer not individually listed in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Foundation Foods. Nutrient values are adapted from the input record cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy non-alcoholic and low-alcohol beer reference entries (FDC ID 173190 and related entries). Energy 18.18 kcal/100 g, protein 0.45 g/100 g, carbohydrate 3.64 g/100 g, fat 0.0 g/100 g are consistent with USDA SR Legacy non-alcoholic beer reference ranges. Fat is confirmed zero by product formulation for this beverage category, consistent with USDA reference data. Values accepted with medium confidence pending direct manufacturer nutrition panel verification.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Big Drop IPA non-alcoholic beer in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 66 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: regular beer (lager), GI approximately 66, as published in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283) and listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. Non-alcoholic beer has a similar fermentable carbohydrate profile to low-alcohol lager. Glycemic load per 330 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI x carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (66 x 12.01) / 100 = 7.93, rounded to 8.0. Carbohydrate per 330 g serving = 3.64 x 3.30 = 12.01 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (3 mg/100 g) and alcohol-free formulation (<0.5% ABV)
hypertension.guidance: Big Drop IPA non-alcoholic beer contains only 3 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 10 mg per 330 ml can) and no alcohol, making it a low-concern beverage for individuals managing hypertension; however, patients should verify the ABV is <0.5% on the can label and limit total fluid intake if fluid restriction applies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (3.64 g/100 g) and low sugar (1.82 g/100 g) with no alcohol-related glycaemic variability
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only 3.64 g carbohydrate and 1.82 g sugar per 100 g, and an estimated glycaemic load of approximately 8 per 330 ml can, Big Drop IPA non-alcoholic beer poses a low glycaemic burden for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; patients should nonetheless account for total carbohydrate intake across all beverages consumed and confirm the product's ABV is <0.5% to ensure alcohol-free status.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, very low sodium, and no alcohol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Big Drop IPA non-alcoholic beer contains no fat, no saturated fat, no cholesterol, and negligible sodium, and eliminates the cardiovascular risk associated with alcohol consumption; it is a low-concern beverage for individuals with cardiovascular disease, though hops-derived polyphenols (xanthohumol) may offer minor vasodilatory benefit with limited clinical evidence.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of meaningful anti-inflammatory nutrients (no omega-3, no significant polyphenols, no fibre, no anti-inflammatory spices)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Big Drop IPA non-alcoholic beer does not meet the positive anti-inflammatory criteria required for RA suitability; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should choose beverages with established anti-inflammatory properties such as green tea or ginger tea in preference to this product.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Big Drop IPA Non-Alcoholic Beer (Can) is a commercially brewed India Pale Ale produced by Big Drop Brewing Co. (United Kingdom) with an alcohol by volume (ABV) of less than 0.5%, classifying it as a non-alcoholic or alcohol-free beer under most regulatory frameworks. The beer is brewed using water, malted barley, hops, and yeast, then processed to remove or prevent the formation of alcohol while retaining the characteristic hop-forward bitterness and aroma of an IPA style. It is packaged in a 330 ml can. The flavour profile is bitter and hoppy with mild malt sweetness, closely mimicking a conventional IPA. Nutritionally, it is very low in calories at approximately 18 kcal per 100 ml, contains minimal carbohydrate (3.64 g/100 g), low sugar (1.82 g/100 g), negligible sodium (3 mg/100 g), and zero fat confirmed by product formulation. It contains gluten from malted barley and is not suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or gluten intolerance. It is suitable for vegetarians and vegans. Clinically, it is a low-concern beverage for hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its very low sodium, low carbohydrate, zero fat, and alcohol-free profile. It does not meet anti-inflammatory criteria for rheumatoid arthritis suitability. Commonly served alongside Western dishes such as burgers and grilled meats in Singapore food delivery contexts.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Big Drop IPA can?
Is Big Drop IPA suitable for diabetics?
Can I drink Big Drop IPA if I have high blood pressure?
Is Big Drop IPA gluten free?
What is the carbohydrate content of Big Drop IPA?
How much sugar is in Big Drop IPA?
Is non-alcoholic beer safe for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy non-alcoholic beer reference ranges and are plausible for the Big Drop IPA product category, but no exact USDA FoodData Central entry exists for this specific branded product. Values are adapted from the input record cross-referenced against USDA SR Legacy non-alcoholic beer references. Medium confidence is assigned pending direct verification against the manufacturer's nutrition information panel on the can label.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — non-alcoholic beer of this type contains no fat by formulation, consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy non-alcoholic beer reference entries. saturated_fat_g and trans_fat_g are confirmed zero by product formulation for the same reason. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero; non-alcoholic beer contains negligible dietary fibre, consistent with USDA reference data. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero; beer contains no cholesterol per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy reference. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown data for this branded non-alcoholic beer is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific branded non-alcoholic IPA beer are not individually reported in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate from regular beer (lager) per University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement exists for Big Drop IPA. Halal status is unconfirmed — while ABV is <0.5%, halal certification status for Big Drop Brewing Co. products sold in Singapore should be verified with MUIS. Country of origin in input record states Singapore; however, Big Drop Brewing Co. is a UK-based company — region_of_origin corrected to United Kingdom. The product may be imported and distributed in Singapore. Nutrient source uses USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (approved secondary global source) as the closest approved reference for this Western packaged beverage; no Singapore HPB or regional primary source covers this branded UK product.
